meta.title: Ergonomics Assessment — Despatch-Bay Carton Lift
meta.subtitle: NIOSH lifting-equation assessment, line-2 packer SEG
meta.author: HSE Manufacturing — Ergonomics
meta.classification: Internal — special-category MSD/fitness data — competent-person (ergonomist / OH professional) review required
meta.deid_notice: All workers are referred to by role / similar-exposure-group (SEG) label. Reported MSD symptoms are reported by SEG with <5 small-cell suppression; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: This ergonomics assessment scores the named despatch-bay carton lift performed by the line-2 packer SEG at Plant 3: cartons are lifted from a floor-level pallet to the conveyor infeed. Each scored task names the task/role and the workstation; reported MSD symptoms are de-identified to SEG and reported with <5 small-cell suppression.

sections.caption: Every score traces to its input parameters; a missing required parameter is a [GAP], never an invented value.

sections.text: Computed score & action band (deterministic engine — ergonomics.to_report_blocks)

sections.caption: The NIOSH multiplier working — the engine's [metrics, table] pair from to_report_blocks(result); RULA/REBA emit their grand/final score + action level instead. The placeholders are filled by the deterministic ergonomics.py call at run time, never narrated.

sections.text: The Lifting Index is interpreted against the NIOSH action band: LI <= 1.0 is within the recommended limit; LI > 1.0 indicates lifting demand exceeds the recommended limit and elevated MSD risk, rising sharply above LI 3.0. The interpretation is cited to the NIOSH equation, never asserted. RULA / REBA results are interpreted against their published action levels (RULA 1-4 / REBA negligible-to-very-high).

rows.control: Raise the pallet on a scissor-lift / spring pallet positioner so the lift origin is at knuckle height (eliminates the floor-level reach)
Provide a vacuum lift-assist for the heavier cartons (mechanical aid)
Rotate packers off the despatch lift on a defined cycle to limit cumulative exposure
Manual-handling technique refresher (supports, does NOT replace, the redesign — training is not a control for a biomechanical overload)

sections.text: A high Lifting Index whose ONLY control is manual-handling training is training-led and inadequate: the task/workstation must be redesigned (positioner / lift-assist) first, or a documented 'higher-order controls not reasonably practicable because…' justification recorded.

items: Re-score the task after the pallet positioner / lift-assist is installed (target LI <= 1.0).
Symptom-triggered review: any reported lower-back or shoulder symptom in the packer SEG triggers a re-assessment (reported by SEG, <5 cells suppressed).
Routine re-assessment on task change or annually, whichever is sooner.

rows.action: Install the spring pallet positioner at the despatch lift origin
Trial a vacuum lift-assist for cartons over 15 kg
Re-score the lift with the engine after the positioner is installed

items: [GAP] — any required NIOSH parameter not captured at intake is recorded here and the score is withheld until it is measured; no parameter is invented.
India site: the Factories Act health-provision return is resolved via hse-india with mandatory state detection; a state return owed is recorded as a [GAP], never a national form number.

sections.text: Decision-support only. The SME review (Chartered Ergonomist (CIEHF) / Occupational-Health Professional) precedes — and never replaces — the human competent-person sign-off. Regulatory basis: ISO 45001 §6.1.2 / §8.1.2 (KB-SNIP-MANUFACTURING-CLAUSE-MAP); NIOSH lifting equation / RULA / REBA / ISO 11228-1/-2/-3 method.
